user_title: Head of Change Execution, WPB Private Banking & Wealth Solutions, Asia Pacific

talking_points.headline: SR 26-2's AI Exclusion Creates an Immediate Governance Action Item for HSBC Change Delivery
talking_points.why_it_matters: Every AI tool Adam's change programme is deploying sits in a regulatory grey zone: SR 26-2 governs model risk but explicitly excludes generative and agentic AI, meaning HSBC must self-govern its fastest-growing class of AI systems before the next exam cycle.
bullets: SR 26-2, issued jointly by the Fed, OCC, and FDIC in April 2026, replaces SR 11-7 but places all generative and agentic AI explicitly outside its model risk governance scope — creating a structural compliance gap at the exact moment banks are scaling these tools

A separate federal RFI on AI system governance is 'planned but not yet issued' — meaning no formal guidance will arrive in H2 2026; change teams must document their own AI governance frameworks now, before examiners begin asking

Adam's AI-enabled change programme must produce a model inventory covering all generative AI tools in deployment or pilot, with human-in-the-loop thresholds, escalation protocols, and audit trails documented before the next supervisory cycle

source_links.title: TechTimes: Bank AI Oversight Expands to Every Exam
Cutover: SR 26-2 and Agentic AI Banking Guide
ValidMind: SR 26-2 What Banks Must Know

talking_points.headline: UBS Targets HSBC's Core APAC Markets with Four Coordinated Senior Hong Kong Appointments
talking_points.why_it_matters: UBS has simultaneously filled four senior wealth front-office roles covering Hong Kong Private Clients, Taiwan International, and Southeast Asia — a coordinated talent push directly into Adam's markets that signals accelerating competitive pressure in the second half of 2026.
bullets: Pierre Widmer, Head of GWM Asia & Switzerland North Asia, UBS takes the senior APAC lead from September 1; Neil Yiu leads Southeast Asia Hong Kong; Simon Chang leads Taiwan International — all markets where Adam's WPB delivery programmes are active

Sherwin Wong, Market Team Head for Private Clients Greater China, UBS joins directly from JPMorgan, demonstrating deliberate cross-competitor talent raids in HSBC's core geographies at a moment when HSBC itself is managing a leadership transition in RBWM Asia

Concurrent with UBS Asset Management's $1.8 trillion platform consolidation eliminating product access friction, UBS is presenting a strengthened end-to-end offer in HSBC's home markets — timing that demands attention from Adam's delivery programme

source_links.title: finews.asia: UBS Expands Wealth Front Office HK

talking_points.headline: HSBC RBWM Asia Head Moves to Global Digital Role — Transition Risk in Adam's Region
talking_points.why_it_matters: The elevation of HSBC's RBWM Asia head to a global digital role is a strategic signal that APAC's delivery model is becoming the group template — but it creates a near-term leadership transition that Adam's change programmes must navigate without losing momentum.
bullets: HSBC's decision to move its RBWM Asia head into a global digital role reflects the group's confidence in Asia's delivery model and digital maturity — and directly raises the profile and scrutiny of Adam's APAC change programmes as the group benchmark

Leadership transitions at RBWM Asia level historically create a 60–90 day period of reprioritisation and governance ambiguity; Adam must ensure continuity sponsors and escalation paths for active programmes are formally documented during the transition

The timing is particularly consequential: Bloomberg projects Hong Kong private wealth AUM to nearly double to $2.6 trillion by 2031, with 93% digital adoption already among HNW investors — HSBC's delivery clock has never been more visible externally

source_links.title: Asian Private Banker: HSBC RBWM Asia to Digital Role
Bloomberg: Hong Kong Wealth Management 2026 Outlook

AI & Technology.title: SR 26-2 Excludes Generative AI: Banks Face Governance Void at Scale
AI & Technology.summary: Federal regulators issued SR 26-2 in April 2026, replacing SR 11-7, but explicitly excluded generative and agentic AI from its model risk governance scope — creating a regulatory grey zone precisely as banks scale these systems enterprise-wide, with a separate federal RFI on AI governance planned but not yet issued.

AI & Technology.title: Banks Hold Finance's Most Powerful AI Dataset — Who Unlocks It Wins
AI & Technology.summary: Banks and fintechs hold decades of structured financial transaction data that represents the most powerful AI training resource in finance; institutions that deploy LLMs and multimodal models against this proprietary data will achieve durable advantages in credit risk, fraud detection, and wealth advisory personalisation.

HSBC News.title: HSBC RBWM Asia Head Elevated to Global Digital Leadership Role
HSBC News.summary: HSBC's head of Retail Banking and Wealth Management for Asia is transitioning to a new global digital leadership role, reflecting the bank's strategy of exporting APAC's digital playbook to its worldwide WPB operations and elevating the region's delivery model as the global template.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Now Sources Over Half of Global Wealth Inflows From Asia
HSBC News.summary: New data confirms HSBC now generates over 50% of its global wealth management net new inflows from Asia Pacific, cementing the region as the primary growth engine for WPB and significantly raising the strategic weight and visibility of APAC delivery execution across the group.

Competitor Intelligence.title: UBS Makes Four Senior Wealth Hires in HK Targeting HSBC's Home Markets
Competitor Intelligence.summary: UBS Global Wealth Management has made four simultaneous senior front-office appointments in Hong Kong, covering Southeast Asia, Taiwan International, Greater China private clients, and Asia-wide regional leadership — a coordinated talent build directly targeting HSBC's core APAC wealth markets.

Competitor Intelligence.title: StanChart Hires Ex-UBS AI Equity Specialist and Ex-JPM APAC FI Head as New CIOs
Competitor Intelligence.summary: Standard Chartered has appointed Sundeep Gantori, formerly UBS's CIO Equity Strategist managing the global AI portfolio, as CIO Equities, and Jonathan Liang, formerly JPMorgan APAC head of fixed income investment specialists, as CIO Fixed Income & FX — deepening its wealth investment engine for the affluent growth push.

Competitor Intelligence.title: UBS Asset Management Unifies $1.8T Platform Across Public and Private Markets
Competitor Intelligence.summary: UBS Asset Management is consolidating all public and private markets investment capabilities under a single $1.8 trillion integrated platform, appointing Sonja Laud as co-head of investments — simplifying product access for wealth clients globally and removing a friction point that historically favoured integrated banking models.

Regulatory & Markets.title: Hong Kong Expands 0% Tax Regime to Family Offices, Digital Assets, Private Credit
Regulatory & Markets.summary: Hong Kong's FSTB is introducing mid-2026 legislation extending the 0% carried interest and performance fee tax concession to UHNW family office single-investor structures, private credit, digital assets, and overseas real estate — eliminating the minimum return benchmark and replacing HKMA certification with institutional self-assessment.

Regulatory & Markets.title: Deloitte APAC Q1 2026: Five Simultaneous Regulatory Changes Across HSBC's Markets
Regulatory & Markets.summary: Deloitte's Q1 2026 Asia Pacific regulatory update identifies governance and accountability as the dominant theme, with simultaneous active rule changes in Japan (cyber risk), Taiwan (incident reporting), Indonesia (IT governance), Singapore (environmental risk management), and Hong Kong (Sustainable Finance Taxonomy Phase 2A).

Regulatory & Markets.title: HKMA Forms Tokenised Bond Expert Group to Formalise HK Digital Bond Market
Regulatory & Markets.summary: The HKMA established a Tokenised Bond Expert Group in June 2026 to accelerate the legal and regulatory framework for tokenised bond issuance and transactions in Hong Kong, with initial discussions reviewing the existing regime and identifying structural gaps to position HK as the leading centre for digital fixed income.

Private Banking & Wealth.title: Bloomberg: Hong Kong Private Wealth AUM to Double to $2.6T by 2031
Private Banking & Wealth.summary: Bloomberg's 2026 Hong Kong wealth management outlook projects private wealth AUM to nearly double to $2.6 trillion by 2031, supported by 93% digital adoption among investors with $100K+ in assets — the highest in Asia — positioning Hong Kong as the defining growth market for HSBC's Asia wealth strategy.

Private Banking & Wealth.title: UBS and Lombard Odier Use DPM to Navigate Asia Generational Wealth Transfer
Private Banking & Wealth.summary: UBS and Lombard Odier are deploying discretionary portfolio management as the primary vehicle to address generational wealth transfer challenges in Asia, with next-generation clients seeking delegated management with personalised mandates as a balanced approach to volatile markets and complex succession needs.
